name: Quick Sourdough French Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine 1 1/2 cups flour and the next five ingredients (wheat germ, sugar, salt, ginger, and yeast). Mix well. Add water, sour cream, and vinegar to the flour mixture. Blend at low speed until moistened; beat for 3 minutes at medium speed. Stir in an additional 2 to 2 1/2 cups flour until the dough pulls cleanly away from the sides of the bowl.
2. On a floured surface, knead the remaining 1/2 to 1 cup flour into the dough until smooth and elastic, about 5 minutes. Place the dough in a greased bowl; cover loosely with plastic wrap and a cloth towel. Let rise in a warm place until light and doubled in size, 25-35 minutes.
3. Grease a large cookie sheet. Punch down the dough several times to remove all air bubbles. Allow it to rest on a counter covered with an inverted bowl for 15 minutes. Divide the dough in half; roll each half into a 14x8-inch rectangle. Starting with the longest side, roll up; pinch edges firmly to seal. Place seam side down on the greased cookie sheet; taper ends to a point. With a sharp knife, make five 1/4-inch deep diagonal slashes on the top of each loaf. Cover; let rise in a warm place until light and doubled in size, about 15 minutes.
4. Preheat oven to 375°F. Bake for 25 minutes. In a small bowl, beat egg white and water. Remove the bread from the oven; brush with egg white mixture. Sprinkle with poppy seeds. Return to oven. Bake an additional 5 to 10 minutes or until loaves sound hollow when lightly tapped. Immediately remove from cookie sheet; cool on wire racks. Makes 2 (17 oz.) loaves. A Pillsbury Bake-off winning recipe, formatted for you by Judy Lausch.
